summary: This is an extremely brief link post where Joel Spolsky recommends Michael Volker's website on Business Basics for Engineers. The post contains no substantive argument or analysis, serving purely as a pointer to an external resource. It reflects the early blogging style of sharing useful links with minimal commentary. The entire post is a single sentence recommendation.
key_points: Joel recommends Michael Volker's 'Business Basics for Engineers' website as a valuable resource
The post exemplifies the early-2000s link-blogging format where bloggers curated useful resources for their audience
It reflects Joel's recurring interest in bridging the gap between engineering and business knowledge
money_quotes: Michael Volker, an entrepreneur in Canada, has a great web site on Business Basics for Engineers.
key_insight: Engineers benefit from understanding business fundamentals, and curating such resources was a core function of early blogging.
